title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: So right away, I'm starting to think that I can no longer get snacks sent to me from my daughter who lives in Korea because they're less than $800. I don't know how this is going to play out yet, but just kind of siphoned it all here. And then some of the statistics here, there's over 30 countries that recently suspended shipments to the United States, 30 countries. That's a big one.

And then the other big number that caught my eyes, 1.36 billion packages were shipped to the United States from other countries in 2024. So that's a lot of shipping and for all that to stop at the end of August. And now these companies are kind of scrambling right now to update their systems to include new charges. So I hear new charges and I think we can pay the new charges. I'm kind of guessing we are being the consumer. Is it going to hurt small business? Is it going to hurt corporate business? There's so many questions I have with this and I guess a lot of those questions won't be answered right off the get-go of course, but down the road we'll see how it affects us. I'm going on to read here. South Korea is going to be affected greatly by this. The K beauty industry in particular is at risk, including direct to customer platforms like yes style, olive, young, and more.

There's a big arena here that's going to be affected by this new change. So I'm kind of curious to see how this is going to go because I think we've all ordered something online before that's worth less than $800 to be shipped to our house. So porters say ending the diminimus rule in the US will stop counterfeit or illegal goods and brings billions of dollars of new tax revenue. Opponents say it raises cost for consumers and hurts small businesses and online sellers. So you can see how this is going to create a little bit of chaos worldwide. Bottom line time will tell. That's all I can say about that, but you know what you guys, you the viewers had great comments after our last episode. You gave us some great insight. So if there's a particular subject that you would like us to cover, please mention it in the comments below and we will do our best to get that out to you. We'd love to hear from you. We appreciate you tuning in. Thank you very much. You have a great day or great night.

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: That kind of got my attention. It goes on to say here postal services in many countries, including Japan, Australia and Mexico, have paused or stopped delivering most packages to the United States. This follows the end of a long-standing United States rule, known as Diminimus exception, that would allow inexpensive parcels worth $800 or less to enter the country duty-free.

There's a big arena here that's going to be affected by this new change. So I'm kind of curious to see how this is going to go because I think we've all ordered something online before that's worth less than $800 to be shipped to our house. So porters say ending the diminimus rule in the US will stop counterfeit or illegal goods and brings billions of dollars of new tax revenue. Opponents say it raises cost for consumers and hurts small businesses and online sellers. So you can see how this is going to create a little bit of chaos worldwide. Bottom line time will tell. That's all I can say about that, but you know what you guys, you the viewers had great comments after our last episode. You gave us some great insight. We appreciate you tuning in. Thank you very much. You have a great day or great night.
